name: TAO Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression fakeout loses acceptance, reclaims direction, then re-expands with confirmation
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing whether compression fakeouts can reverse and re-expand more profitably than plain first-break entries. In a choppy 4h environment, false initial breaks should occur often enough for a reclaim-and-corrected-expansion setup to matter.
invalidation_condition: Abort if fakeouts are too sparse, if confirmation arrives too late after reclaim, or if high-beta noise destroys the edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves the wrong way first, loses acceptance, and then reclaims in the opposite direction with a tradable corrected expansion leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop, false initial breaks are common. TAO should exaggerate that enough to produce meaningful fakeout-to-reexpansion sequences when filtered through reclaim confirmation.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is useful because its larger swings create clearer fakeout amplitude than ETH while remaining slow enough for bar-based management.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO is only noise, fakeouts will not convert into orderly re-expansion and the setup becomes churn.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the fakeout idea is right but confirmation is late, simplify exits and reduce hold time before altering the reclaim condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Patchy but meaningful fakeout reversals where first-target harvesting matters more than long runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h and one cleaner cross-lane produce a tradable number of fakeout-to-reexpansion events with positive post-cost outcomes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or fakeouts that never convert into directional re-expansion.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; compression, reclaim, and re-expansion are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed through moderate compression definitions rather than extreme rarity

validation_basket.why_included: Best stress-test lane for fakeout-to-reexpansion logic in the current basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: A tradable number of fakeout reversals convert into real re-expansion.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The lane is too noisy or fakeouts fail to follow through.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Clear wrong-side breaks that reverse and deliver one corrected directional leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same fakeout mechanism survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH prints the same pattern with lower noise and acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only exists on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner fakeout reversals than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the concept transfers to another expressive alt lane without requiring TAO-specific volatility.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE preserves the fakeout-reexpansion mechanism with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea depends entirely on TAO's volatility profile.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More orderly fakeout repairs than TAO with slightly smaller follow-through.
